title: SACRAMENTO-

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Californians with disabilities will be able to work and save for the future without losing access to essential public benefits starting January 1, 2016.",
    "Most of California's state agencies are at 'high-risk' to cyber-attacks according to a state auditor report.",
    "AB 670 requires California's most vulnerable agencies to receive cybersecurity risk assessments annually.",
    "County Veteran Service Officers (CVSO) connect veterans with the Federal benefits they have earned.",
    "CVSOs save the state money by qualifying and shifting veterans away from state services such as Medi-Cal.",
    "Over 70% of first-time community college students are unprepared for college-level work and need remedial courses.",
    "In Ventura County, only 40% of community college students requiring remedial courses ever finish a college-level course.",
    "AB 770 influenced the inclusion of $60 million to redesign and streamline basic skills courses statewide."
  ]
}
```
